column1: # Tutorial Chapter 4: macros — record macro source as a label, name
# the range with the `\letter` convention so 1-2-3 binds it to
# Alt-letter, run it, then exercise STEP (Alt-F2) single-step debug.
#   - Macro source stored as a label cell, leading apostrophe forces
#     LABEL mode so `{` and `~` survive as literal data.
#   - /Range Name Create with `\M` reserves Alt-M as the launch key.
#   - Alt-M runs the named macro; F5 GOTO inside the macro repositions
#   - Alt-F2 toggles STEP mode; the SST status indicator confirms a
#     paused macro; SPACE single-steps; Esc aborts; Alt-F2 again
# --- Author the macro as a label in A1.
# The leading apostrophe keeps {GOTO}, ~, and Hello~ as label text
# rather than treating them as live entry directives.
# --- Bind the macro to Alt-M with /Range Name Create \M.
# --- Alt-M runs the macro: GOTO C5, then commit "Hello".
# Re-author a small two-action macro: write 1 to E1, 2 to E2, then
# {QUIT}. Three discrete actions makes the SST pause boundary easy to
# --- Toggle STEP mode (Alt-F2). The status line lights STEP.
# --- Fire the macro. STEP parks before the first action — neither
# E1 nor E2 has a value yet. Status line shows SST ("step single-step").
# --- SPACE advances exactly one action: {LET E1, 1} fires, then
# the macro re-pauses ahead of {LET E2, 2}.
# --- Esc aborts the paused macro before E2 ever gets written.
# --- Toggle STEP off so a future macro run completes normally.
